title: The Theatrical Movie Business Is a Disaster, So ‘The Farewell’ Is a Miracle, With Lulu Wang | Interview
format: Stuber and Hobbs & Shaw make the case that the studio comedy is dying; Crawl and The Farewell make the case that word of mouth still works. Plus, Lulu Wang on making The Farewell exactly the way she wanted to.

segments.heading: Stuber, Hobbs & Shaw, and the Studio Comedy's Death Spiral

films.title: Stuber
films.note: Kumail Nanjiani and Dave Bautista's Lethal Weapon riff opened to $8 million and 47% on Rotten Tomatoes; Fennessey had a nice time, almost nobody else showed up.

films.title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before
films.note: Half teen movie, half rom-com, and the one that made Lana Condor and Noah Centineo stars.

films.title: Set It Up
films.note: The Glen Powell one Dobbins calls genuinely great.

films.title: When Harry Met Sally
films.note: The Rewatchables episode sent Fennessey back for a rewatch; still the bar nothing since really clears.

films.title: Two Weeks Notice
films.note: Sandra Bullock and Hugh Grant, a lesser rom-com from a stronger era, complete with an unfortunate Trump cameo.

films.title: Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw
films.note: Fennessey's read: it isn't an action movie with jokes, it's a comedy that happens to have muscles.

films.title: Shazam!
films.note: A teen comedy that happens to be a superhero movie, and does exactly what it needs to for the audience that wanted it.

segments.heading: The Self-Referential Summer Slate
segments.note: everything left on the calendar either leans on nostalgia or comments on its own genre

films.title: Yesterday
films.note: A Beatles jukebox movie riding on unimpeachable IP; both hosts found it a little disappointing anyway.

films.title: Blinded by the Light
films.note: Built around Bruce Springsteen's music the way Yesterday is built around the Beatles — Fennessey's pick as the one exception that might actually work.

films.title: Good Boys
films.note: The comedy both hosts are watching as a bellwether for whether studios keep funding movies like this.

films.title: Charlie's Angels
films.note: Elizabeth Banks's reboot; Dobbins respects it in theory, doesn't need it in practice.

films.title: Clerks
films.note: Where Kevin Smith's View Askewniverse starts.

films.title: Jersey Girl
films.note: One of the movies Smith made after exiting the View Askewniverse, before circling back to it.

films.title: Poms
films.note: The Diane Keaton/Jacki Weaver retirement-home cheerleading movie nobody saw.

segments.heading: Sword of Trust and The Art of Self-Defense Find Their Audiences

films.title: Sword of Trust
films.note: Lynn Shelton directs Marc Maron in a modest indie that found a modest, appropriate audience.

segments.heading: Fox, Disney, and the Movies Stuck in Limbo

films.title: Dark Phoenix
films.note: The other post-merger Fox release Disney didn't quite know what to do with.

films.title: The Art of Racing in the Rain
films.note: Based on the novel about a dog.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Got the NBA Finals trailer push — the tell that a studio is betting on it as an awards contender.

films.title: Spies in Disguise
films.note: Will Smith-voiced animated action comedy, bounced around the release calendar.

segments.heading: Crawl and the Case for Theatrical B-Movies

films.title: Crawl
films.note: Alexandre Aja's alligators-in-a-flooded-crawlspace movie; a B-movie delight where the crowd cheered every gator kill.

films.title: High Tension
films.note: Aja's earlier, messier predecessor.

films.title: Point Blank
films.note: A Netflix shoutout — Anthony Mackie thriller with a house-escape opening set to Black Flag's 'Rise Above.'

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: A24's Sundance hit, Awkwafina's first dramatic role. $350,000 on four screens over the weekend — the year's best per-screen average, even with a New York blackout eating some showtimes.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: One of the alternatives on the shortlist Fennessey's wife passed on.

films.title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
films.note: Also on that shortlist.

segments.heading: Oscar Season Housekeeping
segments.note: titles listeners said got left off last week's Best Picture conversation

films.title: Ad Astra
films.note: James Gray's father-in-space movie, moved to September; he's never actually competed for an Oscar.

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: Taika Waititi, by early word, at his best.

films.title: The Two Popes
films.note: Fernando Meirelles directs Jonathan Pryce and Anthony Hopkins as Pope Francis and Pope Benedict XVI, written by Anthony McCarten.

films.title: City of God
films.note: Meirelles's earlier, much bigger swing.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: Discussed as 'the untitled Noah Baumbach film' — the title hadn't been announced yet.

films.title: The Laundromat
films.note: Soderbergh's Netflix movie, filed under 'exercise' more than awards play.

films.title: The Aeronauts
films.note: Eddie Redmayne and Felicity Jones, built for IMAX.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
The Farewell
films.note: Her second feature, based on the This American Life story about her own family lying to her dying grandmother about the diagnosis.

films.title: Raise the Red Lantern
films.note: A Zhang Yimou film her parents took her to as a kid in Miami; terrifying at seven or eight, reassessed years later in a college world-cinema class.

films.title: The Piano
films.note: Jane Campion — one of the films that made her want to direct.

films.title: Secretary
films.note: The other: a movie about S&M she found unexpectedly romantic and earnest.

films.title: Posthumous
films.note: Her directorial debut, a screwball rom-com she and a first-time producer figured out how to make with no industry connections.

films.title: Touch
films.note: Her short film, made through Film Independent's Project Involve — the first place she tried mixing comedy with a dark subject.

Unproduced: Children of the New World (Wang's next film, in development)

To All the Boys I've Loved Before.title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before

The Art of Racing in the Rain.title: The Art of Racing in the Rain

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
